title: Replaceable You

jokes.text: Gas!
jokes.setup: Opening moments suggesting someone passing gas or being startled by gas
jokes.punchline: The blunt exclamation
jokes.explanation: Simple bodily function humor, minimal craft but economical

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer encountering some mishap or realization
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase perfectly integrated with Homer's character - became a cultural phenomenon

jokes.text: Oh, did you pick that big scab off your knee?
jokes.setup: Lisa asks if Bart wants to see something cool
jokes.punchline: Bart assumes it's gross bodily function rather than science
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's grossout tendencies vs Lisa's academic interests

jokes.text: My science fair project proves that an asteroid will strike the earth on July 15, 9789.
jokes.setup: Lisa reveals her science project
jokes.punchline: The incredibly specific but impossibly distant date
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of scientific precision with ultimate irrelevance - very Lisa

jokes.text: I'll be President of Hell by then.
jokes.setup: Lisa's asteroid prediction in 7,000+ years
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual assumption he'll rule Hell by then
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart line - dark humor delivered with childlike casualness, highly quotable

jokes.text: A fisteroid hitting the planet Nerd!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to come up with a science project
jokes.punchline: Crude portmanteau combining 'fist' and 'asteroid' targeting nerds
jokes.explanation: Juvenile wordplay that fits Bart but relies on crude humor rather than wit

jokes.text: My dog and I accidentally touched tongues.
jokes.setup: Milhouse needs a cootie shot
jokes.punchline: The disturbing reason why
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor that establishes Milhouse's awkwardness

jokes.text: How is it 'accidentally' when this is the fifth time?
jokes.setup: Milhouse claims accidental tongue touching with dog
jokes.punchline: Reveals this is a pattern, not an accident
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that transforms accident into disturbing habit

jokes.text: That was preventative. This is morning-after.
jokes.setup: Marge notes similarity to last year's cootie patch
jokes.punchline: Bart distinguishes using medical/birth control terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever application of adult medical terminology to childhood nonsense

jokes.text: Good ideas don't grow on trees. Hmm. Or do they?
jokes.setup: Bart lamenting lack of ideas
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's literally looking at trees, subverting the idiom
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of common idiom with visual element

jokes.text: Not really. I was trying to bounce it off your left testie.
jokes.setup: Martin compliments Bart's basketball shot
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals he was actually aiming for Martin's testicle
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart cruelty with mild shock value

jokes.text: 'Testis,' my friend.
jokes.setup: Bart uses crude term 'testie'
jokes.punchline: Martin pedantically corrects to proper medical term
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character moment - pedantic even about his own genitals

jokes.text: To be a mathlete! Without the 'M.'
jokes.setup: Bart offers to pick Martin first for basketball
jokes.punchline: Martin's dorky excitement about being an 'athlete'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Martin's nerd identity

jokes.text: Fly, orb, and find thy hoop!
jokes.setup: Martin attempting to play basketball
jokes.punchline: His overwrought, Shakespearean approach to shooting
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Martin can't even play basketball normally

jokes.text: A woman of stature!
jokes.setup: Roz introduces herself as Homer's new assistant
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward attempt at a compliment
jokes.explanation: Mild Homer awkwardness - tries to be formal but sounds weird

jokes.text: I'm just a gal from Ohio who never wants to call her daddy again and say, 'I need to borrow money.'
jokes.setup: Homer asks about Roz's background
jokes.punchline: Overly specific motivation rooted in family shame
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment with relatable financial anxiety

jokes.text: So your dad has money to lend?
jokes.setup: Roz explains she doesn't want to borrow money from her dad
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the point and focuses on dad's wealth
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - misses emotional subtext entirely

jokes.text: Just leave a little at the bottom, so someone else has to make the next pot. The burning smell will let them know.
jokes.setup: Showing Homer the coffee routine
jokes.punchline: Deliberately passive-aggressive office behavior
jokes.explanation: Sharp workplace satire - the calculated pettiness rings very true

jokes.text: Oh, Simpson, you do know how to whip my buggy.
jokes.setup: Homer pushes Burns in wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Burns' inappropriate double entendre
jokes.explanation: Typical Burns creepiness but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: That sucker soaks up flattery like a Twinkie soaks up gravy.
jokes.setup: Observing Burns' reaction to compliments
jokes.punchline: Bizarre but vivid simile
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird and specific simile that's both gross and accurate

jokes.text: Your giggle's like strawberry wine.
jokes.setup: Roz manipulating Burns with compliments
jokes.punchline: Overly romantic description of his giggle
jokes.explanation: Good manipulation humor, absurd romantic language for Burns

jokes.text: And your snort's like a summer breeze.
jokes.setup: Continuing romantic compliments to Burns
jokes.punchline: Making even his snort sound poetic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the flattery bit

jokes.text: Thy will be done, Lord Vader.
jokes.setup: Bart and Martin looking for robopet inspiration
jokes.punchline: Random Star Wars toy gives Bart the idea
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much twist

jokes.text: Hey, what if we make some kind of robopet?
jokes.setup: Bart and Martin need a science project idea
jokes.punchline: Bart's 'original' idea comes immediately after seeing a robotic toy
jokes.explanation: Visual joke showing Bart's 'creativity' is just copying what he sees

jokes.text: Where? Is it Nelson?
jokes.setup: Martin says 'Bully, Bart! Bully!' as encouragement
jokes.punchline: Bart thinks there's an actual bully present
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection based on Bart's school trauma and Martin's vocabulary

jokes.text: Research shows it's impossible to gaze at a baby seal and say anything but... Aw...
jokes.setup: Martin explaining their robopet design strategy
jokes.punchline: The scientifically observed universal 'aw' reaction
jokes.explanation: Good Martin logic - applying science to cute manipulation

jokes.text: And at science fairs, the 'aw's get the 'A's.
jokes.setup: Explaining the baby seal reaction
jokes.punchline: Clever rhyme connecting emotion to grades
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with educational satire - perfectly economical

jokes.text: Heavens to Asimov!
jokes.setup: Martin reacting to something surprising
jokes.punchline: Nerd version of 'Heavens to Betsy' referencing Isaac Asimov
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin exclamation - updates classic phrase with sci-fi reference

jokes.text: Get my seal club! The big one! They're all pretty big.
jokes.setup: Homer sees the robotic seal, wants his club
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual note that all his clubs are big
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about seal clubbing with Marge's matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: So the soft fur, big eyes, and gentle motion are designed to make it one hell of a pet, with none of the poop.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining their robopet's appeal
jokes.punchline: Crude but practical benefit of no waste
jokes.explanation: Good practical kid logic - cuts to what really matters about pets

jokes.text: Unfortunately, due to budget cuts, we could not afford our third place ribbon.
jokes.setup: Science fair awards ceremony
jokes.punchline: School so broke they can't buy a third place ribbon
jokes.explanation: Good satire of underfunded public schools

jokes.text: They're all the same, Gloomhilda.
jokes.setup: Lisa corrects that her project is about asteroids not fossil fuels
jokes.punchline: Judge dismisses her with cruel nickname
jokes.explanation: Mean but funny dismissal of Lisa's earnest environmental concerns

jokes.text: I carried you in my belly for four and a half months. When you fell out on the bus, that's when my job ended.
jokes.setup: Judge talking about humans failing to provide comfort
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely personal overshare about premature birth
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully inappropriate and specific trauma dump from random character

jokes.text: You must've baked a mighty fine pie.
jokes.setup: Lisa got a ribbon at science fair
jokes.punchline: Homer assumes it was for baking, not science
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to his daughter's interests

jokes.text: I'm my own nerd!
jokes.setup: Lisa complaining Bart had help from a nerd
jokes.punchline: Proud declaration of her self-sufficient nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - defensive pride in her intellectual independence

jokes.text: Look at poor old Jasper over there.
jokes.setup: Homer consoling Lisa about not being first
jokes.punchline: Pointing to elderly man as example of worse fate
jokes.explanation: Sets up the visual gag of Jasper's pathetic situation

jokes.text: He lost his wife, his kids don't call. Soon he'll just be bones and beard.
jokes.setup: Homer pointing out Jasper as worse off than Lisa
jokes.punchline: Brutally matter-of-fact description of elderly decline
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny aging observation with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Soon he'll just be bones and beard.
jokes.setup: Jasper's sad life circumstances
jokes.punchline: Grotesque prediction of his physical deterioration
jokes.explanation: Vivid and disturbing image that escalates the dark humor effectively

jokes.text: Why would a Homer Humiliator do this to me?
jokes.setup: Homer is apparently being humiliated by Bart's device
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused question about why his own humiliator would humiliate him
jokes.explanation: Great callback with Homer's obliviousness to the device's obvious purpose

jokes.text: Life imitates Flart. It says that on the poster. I thought of it first.
jokes.setup: Homer describing a movie experience
jokes.punchline: Claims credit for obvious parody title he didn't create
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer delusion taking credit for others' work

jokes.text: Guess what happens when a mop is replaced with an octopus. I'll give you a clue. It's funny!
jokes.setup: Homer describing movie plot
jokes.punchline: His terrible attempt to build suspense about obvious sight gag
jokes.explanation: Homer's bad storytelling skills on display

jokes.text: Someone snitched? W-W-What are you going to do to me? You're demoted. Up, or down? Down! Don't tell my wife.
jokes.setup: Burns confronts Homer about going to movies
jokes.punchline: Homer's rapid-fire panic and final plea
jokes.explanation: Good escalating panic from Homer with realistic workplace anxiety

jokes.text: I love when you're sleeping and at peace.
jokes.setup: Martin took a photo of sleeping Bart
jokes.punchline: Creepily romantic explanation
jokes.explanation: Creepy Martin behavior that's funny because it's so inappropriate

jokes.text: Unless you got any heroin. You got any heroin?
jokes.setup: Visitor asking for more robot pets for nursing home
jokes.punchline: Casual request for hard drugs as alternative
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about nursing home conditions and substance abuse

jokes.text: You're at Moe's tavern-- lady-free since '83.
jokes.setup: Moe reassuring Homer about avoiding women
jokes.punchline: Proud declaration of decades-long female absence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe boast about his bar's complete lack of female customers

jokes.text: This is a gentleman's club! Yeah! So, what you boys drinkin'? I'm buyin'.
jokes.setup: Roz enters right after Moe's lady-free boast
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks his streak and takes charge
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback subversion with role reversal

jokes.text: My job is the reason I get the checks every week.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his professional crisis to Marge
jokes.punchline: Profound realization of basic employment concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer moment - treats obvious fact as revelation

jokes.text: Not in physical size, but...
jokes.setup: Homer explaining smaller paychecks
jokes.punchline: Clarifies the checks aren't physically smaller
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer literal thinking about abstract concepts

jokes.text: If we don't have a frickin' funeral soon, people, you can say good-bye to Howard's Flowers!
jokes.setup: Funeral home meeting about lack of business
jokes.punchline: Florist's desperate need for deaths to continue business
jokes.explanation: Dark economic humor about death industry dependence

jokes.text: Hard to believe two ten-year-old kids built this. And I heard one of them did all the work.
jokes.setup: Admiring the robots
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment that Martin did everything
jokes.explanation: Good acknowledgment of the obvious collaboration inequality

jokes.text: And I heard one of them did all the work.
jokes.setup: Praising the robot built by two kids
jokes.punchline: Implying Bart didn't contribute, which is probably true
jokes.explanation: Acknowledges the obvious dynamic between Bart and Martin's partnership

jokes.text: Since I got demoted, I have to show my I.D., but the picture was taken so long ago, I put on a lot of weight, so I have to stretch out my face and say, 'I used to look like this.'
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he's late
jokes.punchline: Elaborate physical routine to match old photo
jokes.explanation: Relatable aging humor with specific absurd physical detail

jokes.text: Can one of the hundred be making the list? Also, no.
jokes.setup: Roz demands list of 100 ways to make Homer's job worse
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempt to include the list-making itself
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic trying to game the system

jokes.text: I-I'll have to climb your Grand Tetons another night.
jokes.setup: Frink canceling date with Miss Wyoming
jokes.punchline: Geographic double entendre about her breasts
jokes.explanation: Standard geographic innuendo, fits Frink's nerdy approach to sexuality

jokes.text: The breast joke! I-I served it up. Why won't he love me?!
jokes.setup: Martin made a breast pun about Grand Tetons
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea for approval after crude joke attempt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin moment - desperate for acceptance through inappropriate humor

jokes.text: She's the most evil person to come out of Ohio since LeBron.
jokes.setup: Homer describing Roz's evil nature
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to LeBron James leaving Cleveland
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to sports controversy, reasonably clever comparison

jokes.text: She's the most evil person to come out of Ohio since LeBron.
jokes.setup: Homer describing evil Roz
jokes.punchline: Compares her to LeBron James leaving Cleveland
jokes.explanation: Topical sports reference with good escalation

jokes.text: But at least he thought long and hard before screwing everybody over, in public, for no reason.
jokes.setup: Comparing Roz to LeBron
jokes.punchline: Backhanded defense of LeBron's thoughtful betrayal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the sports comparison with ironic praise

jokes.text: Are you aware that 'stones' means man junk?
jokes.setup: Ned using the word 'stones'
jokes.punchline: Homer's mortified realization about the euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's delayed understanding of crude language

jokes.text: Are you aware that 'stones' means man junk?
jokes.setup: Flanders used the word 'stones'
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude interpretation and embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Homer's crude misunderstanding of innocent phrase

jokes.text: What the-- I've been using that around the boys.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes 'stones' has crude meaning
jokes.punchline: Horrified realization of past inappropriate usage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's embarrassment

jokes.text: I can't stand to be touched! Never tell anyone!
jokes.setup: Flashback to Roz at Bible camp
jokes.punchline: Her phobia revealed as manipulative weakness
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation that explains her manipulation and provides Homer's weapon

jokes.text: Hail Flanders! Mightier than Jesus!
jokes.setup: Ned helping Homer with his Roz problem
jokes.punchline: Homer's blasphemous overstatement of gratitude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer excess - elevating Ned above Jesus for practical help

jokes.text: I hereby call to order this meeting of the North American Man-Bot Love Association. I'll say this one more time: we really should change that name.
jokes.setup: Frink calling meeting to order
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging their organization name sounds inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about unfortunate acronym

jokes.text: I'll say this one more time: we really should change that name.
jokes.setup: North American Man-Bot Love Association meeting
jokes.punchline: Ongoing concern about the sexual implications of the name
jokes.explanation: Running gag about unfortunate organization name with sexual connotations

jokes.text: as long as 'Man-Bot Love' is in there somewhere.
jokes.setup: Hibbert suggesting name change
jokes.punchline: Frink insisting on keeping the inappropriate part
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Frink's obliviousness to inappropriate connotations

jokes.text: Oh, it's always busy when I call.
jokes.setup: Wiggum tries to call 911
jokes.punchline: Can't get through to his own emergency service
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum incompetence - can't reach his own department

jokes.text: Who told you? I won't say, but his initials are S.F. Stupid Flanders!
jokes.setup: Roz asks who told Homer her secret
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives away the answer while claiming secrecy
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of trying to keep a secret

jokes.text: I underestimated you. I don't know what that middle word means.
jokes.setup: Roz admits she underestimated Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't understand 'estimated'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vocabulary limitation at moment of triumph

jokes.text: I underestimated you. I don't know what that middle word means.
jokes.setup: Roz admitting she underestimated Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion about the word 'underestimated'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vocabulary limitation - proving her point about underestimation

jokes.text: Rub the butter around you. Need more butter! And not the unsalted.
jokes.setup: Jasper apparently stuck somewhere needing help
jokes.punchline: Specific butter requirements for escape
jokes.explanation: Absurd emergency with oddly specific butter preferences

jokes.text: Mr. J., if I don't get my green card, they're gonna turn me back into a washing machine.
jokes.setup: Robot escaping from prison
jokes.punchline: Immigration fears expressed through appliance transformation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on immigration anxiety through robot perspective

jokes.text: If I don't get my green card, they're gonna turn me back into a washing machine.
jokes.setup: Robot worried about immigration status
jokes.punchline: Threat of being converted back to appliance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on immigration anxiety with robot twist

jokes.text: 'Cause there's no stronger force than a senior citizen holding onto a freebie.
jokes.setup: Seniors reunited with robot pets
jokes.punchline: Universal truth about elderly and free stuff
jokes.explanation: Perfect observational humor about senior citizen behavior

tmdb_overview: Lisa's science fair project is overshadowed by Bart's mechanical baby seal; Homer's new assistant ends up stealing his job.
